cases.note: canonical NaCl box vector (NaCl distribution tests/box): nonce||box authenticates and opens to the known 131-byte plaintext
peer-symmetric: Bob's secret + Alice's public derive the same shared key (nacl.box.before) and open the same bundle to the same plaintext
tampered ciphertext (last byte flipped) fails Poly1305 authentication -> null
tampered nonce (first byte flipped) changes the keystream so the tag no longer verifies -> null
wrong shared key (peerPublicKey set to Alice's own public) derives a valid but different box key -> null
bundle one byte below the nonce(24)+overhead(16)=40 minimum is rejected by the length guard before any crypto -> null
empty bundle is below the minimum length -> null
minimum-length 40-byte all-zero bundle passes the length guard but fails authentication -> null
empty message seals to nonce||tag (deterministic encrypt pin)
single byte message
utf-8 text message
1KB message
all-0xFF nonce (max) seals fine
a 23-byte nonce is rejected (null) on both sides
keypair derives deterministically from a seed (X25519 clamped)
second seed derives the matching peer keypair
a 0..31 sequential seed clamps like tweetnacl
the encrypt-pin hello bundle opens back to its plaintext (cross-arm)
alice->bob shared key
bob->alice derives the same key
alice->carol distinct key
seal under alice/bob key, fixed nonce
seal empty message
bad nonce length -> null
open the alice/bob bundle back to plaintext
tampered bundle -> null
wrong key (alice/carol) -> null
too-short bundle -> null
